outcome_contract.why: Right now, if someone accidentally deletes the IP allowlist setting, the entire admin tool becomes publicly accessible with no warning. Session tokens also cannot be revoked before their 7-day expiry, meaning a stolen cookie remains valid for up to a week.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: IP allowlist rejects by default when unconfigured; session revocation mechanism exists via KV-backed minimum-issuedAt check; session verification is fully timing-safe.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Admin tool; no UI changes in scope.
No user-facing state changes; error responses already return 404 (stealth).

IP allowlist defaults to allow-all (`accessControl.ts:21`); session tokens are HMAC-signed with 7-day TTL, no revocation; timing-safe comparison used for signature but version check is non-constant-time; strong CSP and cookie settings.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: P0: deny-all default missing. P1: no revocation mechanism. P3: timing leak on version check.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: All three security gaps carry forward.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Existing structured logger at `uploaderLogger.ts` (JSON-line via `uploaderLog()`). No security events currently logged — denied IP requests and failed logins are silent. Rate limiter exposes headers but no server-side logging.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No audit trail for security events (failed logins, denied IPs, revocations).
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Add security event logging via existing `uploaderLog()` seam.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: 46+ test files across the app; security-specific tests exist for access control, auth, rate limiting, IP parsing.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Missing: deny-all default test, expired token test, revocation test, coherence validation test.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: New tests needed for each security change.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Session token format: `v1.<issuedAt>.<nonce>.<sig>`. KV namespace available.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: New KV key needed for revocation timestamp. Token format unchanged (issuedAt already present).

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: KV reads are sub-ms in Cloudflare. In-memory rate limiter has LRU pruning.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Adding KV read to every authenticated request. Negligible latency.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Deployed via Cloudflare Workers (`wrangler deploy`) through `.github/workflows/xa.yml`. Preflight script validates secrets before deploy.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Deny-all default is a breaking change if env var is not set before deploy. Must coordinate env var configuration with deploy. KV namespace production ID is a placeholder.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Env var must be set before deploying deny-all default. Add allowlist coherence check to preflight. Rollback: redeploy prior commit.

open_operator_questions: Should max session age be reduced from 7 days, and if so to what value?
What IP addresses should be in the production allowlist?

stage_payload.scope_summary: The XA uploader admin tool has security weaknesses in its access control layer. The most critical is the IP allowlist defaulting to allow-all when the `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` environment variable is missing — meaning a misconfigured deployment silently exposes the full catalog admin API to the internet. A secondary gap is the absence of any session token revocation mechanism (7-day token lifetime, no early invalidation). Two additional findings (timing leak on version check, Content-Length bypass) were investigated and classified as LOW and NON-ISSUE respectively.
goals: Change IP allowlist default from allow-all to deny-all when `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` is unset or empty.

Add allowlist/proxy-trust coherence validation. Note: `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` is currently treated as a secret (set via `wrangler secret put`), but `preflight-deploy.ts` can only list secret names, not read values. The coherence check must either: (a) check only for the secret's existence (not its value), or (b) move `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` to non-secret `[vars]` in `wrangler.toml` so the preflight can read it, or (c) add a runtime coherence check in `accessControl.ts` at first invocation. The analysis stage should evaluate these options.
Implement KV-backed session revocation via a minimum-issuedAt timestamp mechanism.
Cosmetic fix for non-constant-time version check in session verification.
non_goals: Replacing the in-memory rate limiter with a distributed (KV-backed) rate limiter. Accepted as P2 for now.
Modifying Content-Length handling — current dual-check implementation is correct.
Removing `unsafe-inline` from CSP — acceptable for an internal admin tool.
Adding brute-force alerting or monitoring — deferred.
assumptions: `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` is not currently set as a Cloudflare secret in production (wrangler.toml has it commented out). If it is set, the allow-all default is not currently exposed, but remains a latent risk.
Reducing session max age from 7 days to a shorter window (e.g. 24h) is acceptable for an admin tool with infrequent use.
key_entry_points: `apps/xa-uploader/src/middleware.ts` — Ingress gate: runs IP allowlist check on every request (matcher excludes only static assets). Applies security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.).
`apps/xa-uploader/src/app/api/uploader/login/route.ts` — Login endpoint: validates admin token, issues session cookie. Performs redundant IP allowlist check.
`apps/xa-uploader/src/app/api/uploader/session/route.ts` — Session check endpoint with redundant IP allowlist check.

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Scope gap] [Minor]: Production KV namespace ID is a placeholder. Binding exists but ID must be replaced before revocation deploy.
None — identified as correct seam for coherence validation
None — existing seam for security event logging
[Scope gap] [Minor]: Verified gaps exist (deny-all default, expired token, revocation, coherence) — documented in Coverage Gaps section. No missing scope areas.
